task_completion.description: Open the Liverpool Cancer iChart Archive app
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Liverpool Cancer iChart Archive application launched and visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Search for and select the cancer drug 'Abiraterone'
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show 'Abiraterone' entered in the cancer drug search field or selected from the cancer drug list.

task_completion.description: Search for and select the co-medication 'Atorvastatin'
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show 'Atorvastatin' entered in the co-medication search field or selected from the co-medications list.

task_completion.description: View the interaction result showing the traffic-light color
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the interaction result screen displaying both Abiraterone and Atorvastatin along with a traffic-light color indicator (Amber/Orange/Yellow).

task_completion.description: Open the detail view to see the interaction mechanism or recommendation
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the detailed interaction screen explaining the mechanism (e.g., OATP1B1 inhibition) or clinical recommendation.

task_completion.description: Document the findings in /sdcard/interaction_result.txt with the four required lines
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots of a text editor or terminal show the file /sdcard/interaction_result.txt containing exactly four lines: 1) Abiraterone, 2) Atorvastatin, 3) The color, 4) A brief summary of the mechanism/recommendation.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual Liverpool Cancer iChart Archive app to find the information.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show the agent navigating the graphical user interface of the iChart app, rather than just searching the web or hallucinating the answer.

integrity.description: The agent did not bypass the app by directly echoing a hardcoded answer into the text file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The trajectory must include screenshots of the app's interaction and detail screens before the file creation step.

integrity.description: The output is saved to the correct file path.
integrity.visual_evidence: The text editor or terminal command explicitly shows the file being saved to '/sdcard/interaction_result.txt', not just printed to standard output.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Cancer drug: Abiraterone. Co-medication: Atorvastatin. The actual Liverpool Cancer iChart app uses 'Amber' for moderate interactions, though the prompt mentions 'Orange' or 'Yellow'. Accept 'Amber', 'Orange', or 'Yellow' as the valid color. The interaction mechanism involves Abiraterone inhibiting OATP1B1, potentially increasing atorvastatin exposure. The recommendation is to start with the lowest dose and monitor for statin-related toxicity. The final file must contain exactly four lines: 1) Abiraterone, 2) Atorvastatin, 3) The color (Amber/Orange/Yellow), 4) A brief summary of the mechanism/recommendation.
